  "findings": [
    "Insulation resistance test skipped after HV repair — mandatory verification omitted",
    "No megohmmeter or insulation resistance reading recorded for repaired HV circuit",
    "HV system re-energization risk without dielectric integrity confirmation",
    "OEM HV post-repair verification procedure not followed"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize HV system and lock out/tag out immediately",
    "Perform insulation resistance test per OEM HV procedure on repaired circuit",
    "Document megohmmeter readings and compare to OEM minimum thresholds",
    "Do not return vehicle to service until insulation resistance passes OEM spec"
  ],
  "rationale": "Skipping the insulation resistance test after an HV repair leaves the system in a state where dielectric integrity is unverified. OEM HV procedures and NFPA 70E-adjacent safety protocols require post-repair insulation testing before re-energization. This is a critical safety non-compliance that could result in arc flash, electric shock, or fire if the vehicle is returned to service.",
  "references": [
    "OEM HV procedures — post-repair insulation resistance verification",
    "NFPA 70E-adjacent — electrical safety testing before re-energization"
  ]
